steps.title: Find the greatest common factor (GCF)
steps.content:
To factor, we first find what divides both terms:
  • Coefficients: 158 = 2 × 79 and 24 = 2 × 12, so GCF of coefficients is 2
  • Variables: The highest power of $t$ dividing both $t^3$ and $t^2u$ is $t^2$
  • The overall GCF is $2t^2$

steps.title: Factor out the GCF and select the answer
steps.content:
Divide each term by $2t^2$:
First term: $\frac{158t^3}{2t^2} = 79t$
Second term: $\frac{24t^2u}{2t^2} = 12u$
$$158t^3 - 24t^2u = 2t^2(79t - 12u)$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
